# Clintela Engineering Review

**Comprehensive architecture and implementation guide**

*Generated by /plan-eng-review on 2026-03-17*

## Executive Summary

This document captures the engineering review of the Clintela Foundation project, including architectural decisions, system diagrams, test plans, and implementation guidance. All major architectural decisions have been resolved and are ready for implementation.

**Review Status:** ✅ Complete
**Architecture Decisions:** 7 resolved
**Critical Gaps:** 0
**Unresolved Issues:** 0

## System Architecture

```

│  │              │  │              │  │              │            │
│  │ • Web Chat   │  │ • Dashboard  │  │ • Metrics    │            │
│  │ • SMS        │  │ • Real-time  │  │ • Analytics  │            │
│  │ • Voice      │  │ • Triage     │  │ • Reports    │            │

└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Component Responsibilities

| Component | Technology | Responsibility |
|-----------|------------|----------------|
| Patient Interface | Django Templates + HTMX | SMS, web chat, voice interaction |
| Clinician Interface | Django Templates + HTMX + WebSockets | Real-time dashboard, triage view |
| Admin Interface | Django Templates | Metrics, analytics, reporting |
| Agent System | LangChain/LangGraph | Multi-agent orchestration |
| Patient Models | Django ORM | Patient, caregiver, encounter data |
| Clinical Content | PostgreSQL JSONB | Pathways, templates, milestones |
| Ollama Cloud | External API | LLM inference and embeddings |
| Twilio | External API | SMS and voice communication |
| PostgreSQL | Database | All persistent data storage |

## Agent Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    AGENT ORCHESTRATION FLOW                      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│   INPUT                    SUPERVISOR                    OUTPUT │
│     │                          │                             │
│     ▼                          ▼                             │
│ ┌────────┐              ┌──────────────┐              ┌────────┐ │
│ │Patient │─────────────►│   SUPERVISOR │─────────────►│Patient │ │
│ │Message │              │    AGENT     │              │Response│ │
│ └────────┘              └──────┬───────┘              └────────┘ │
│                                │                             │
│                    ┌─────────────┼─────────────┐              │
│                    │             │             │              │
│                    ▼             ▼             ▼              │
│            ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────┐           │
│            │  CARE    │ │  NURSE   │ │  SPECIAL │           │
│            │COORDINATOR│ │  TRIAGE  │ │   IST    │           │
│            │   AGENT  │ │   AGENT  │ │  AGENTS  │           │
│            └──────────┘ └──────────┘ └──────────┘           │
│                    │             │             │              │
│                    └─────────────┼─────────────┘              │
│                                  │                           │
│                                  ▼                           │
│                         ┌──────────────┐                    │
│                         │ DOCUMENTATION│                    │
│                         │    AGENT     │                    │
│                         └──────────────┘                    │
│                                                                  │
│  RULES:                                                          │
│  • Supervisor routes all requests                                │
│  • Specialists are tools, not autonomous                         │
│  • All agent decisions logged for audit                          │
│  • Human approval gates on escalations                           │
│                                                                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Agent Responsibilities

| Agent | Role | Capabilities |
|-------|------|--------------|
| **Supervisor** | Central router | Determines which agent handles request, manages escalation decisions, coordinates multi-agent workflows |
| **Care Coordinator** | Patient-facing | Translates clinical guidance to patient-friendly language, asks follow-up questions, delivers reminders and surveys |
| **Nurse Triage** | Clinical assessment | Interprets recovery context, classifies symptoms, answers post-op questions, recommends interventions |
| **Documentation** | Record keeping | Creates structured summaries, handoff notes, chart-ready drafts |
| **Cardiology** | Specialist consult | Cardiac-specific recovery guidance (placeholder) |
| **Social Work** | Specialist consult | Social determinants of health, resource connection (placeholder) |
| **Nutrition** | Specialist consult | Dietary guidance for recovery (placeholder) |
| **PT/Rehab** | Specialist consult | Physical therapy and rehabilitation guidance (placeholder) |
| **Palliative** | Specialist consult | Symptom management and comfort care (placeholder) |
| **Pharmacy** | Specialist consult | Medication adherence and interactions (placeholder) |

## Data Flows

### Flow 1: Patient Message → Agent Response

```
┌─────────┐     ┌──────────┐     ┌──────────┐     ┌──────────┐
│ Patient │────►│  Django  │────►│ Supervisor│────►│  Care    │
│ Sends   │     │  Receive │     │  Agent    │     │ Coordinator│
│ Message │     │  Message │     │  Routes   │     │  Agent     │
└─────────┘     └──────────┘     └──────────┘     └────┬─────┘
                                                        │
                              ┌─────────────────────────┘
                              │
                              ▼
                       ┌──────────────┐
                       │   Ollama     │
                       │   Cloud      │
                       │   (LLM API)  │
                       └──────┬───────┘
                              │
                              ▼
                       ┌──────────────┐
                       │   Response   │
                       │   Generated  │
                       └──────┬───────┘
                              │
                              ▼
                       ┌──────────────┐
                       │   Twilio     │
                       │   (Send SMS) │
                       └──────────────┘
```

**Data stored at each step:**
1. **Django Receive**: Raw message, patient ID, timestamp, channel (SMS/web/voice)
2. **Supervisor Agent**: Routing decision, intent classification, confidence score
3. **Care Coordinator**: Conversation context, tool invocations, LLM prompts
4. **Ollama Cloud**: Prompts and responses (not stored by Clintela)
5. **Response Generated**: Generated text, tokens used, latency metrics
6. **Twilio Send**: Delivery status, message SID, cost

### Flow 2: Status Change → Real-time Update

```
Patient reports symptom
         │
         ▼
┌─────────────────┐
│ Nurse Triage    │
│ Agent evaluates │
└────────┬────────┘
         │
         ▼
┌─────────────────┐
│ Status changes  │
│ (green→yellow) │
└────────┬────────┘
         │
         ▼
┌─────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────┐
│ Persist to DB   │────►│ Add to notification│
│ (patients table)│     │ queue            │
└─────────────────┘     └────────┬────────┘
                               │
                               ▼
                        ┌─────────────────┐
                        │ WebSocket       │
                        │ broadcast to    │
                        │ clinicians      │
                        └─────────────────┘
```

### Flow 3: Caregiver Invitation Flow

```
Patient invites caregiver
         │
         ▼
┌─────────────────┐
│ Generate invite │
│ token           │
└────────┬────────┘
         │
         ▼
┌─────────────────┐
│ Send SMS/email  │
│ to caregiver    │
└────────┬────────┘
         │
         ▼
┌─────────────────┐
│ Caregiver       │
│ accepts with    │
│ DOB verification│
└────────┬────────┘
         │
         ▼
┌─────────────────┐
│ Record consent  │
│ in DB           │
└────────┬────────┘
         │
         ▼
┌─────────────────┐
│ Caregiver can   │
│ view patient    │
│ data (read-only)│
└─────────────────┘
```

## State Machines

### Patient Status State Machine

```
┌─────────┐    ┌─────────┐    ┌─────────┐    ┌─────────┐
│  GREEN  │───►│ YELLOW  │───►│ ORANGE  │───►│   RED   │
│ (Info)  │    │(Notice) │    │(Warning)│    │(Critical)│
└────┬────┘    └────┬────┘    └────┬────┘    └────┬────┘
     │              │              │              │
     │              │              │              │
     └──────────────┴──────────────┴──────────────┘
                    │
                    ▼
            ┌──────────────┐
            │  RESOLVED    │
            │(Recovery     │
            │ complete)     │
            └──────────────┘

Transitions:
- GREEN → YELLOW: Patient reports mild symptom
- YELLOW → ORANGE: Symptom persists or worsens
- ORANGE → RED: Critical symptom or no response
- Any → RESOLVED: Recovery complete or patient discharged
- Any → GREEN: Issue resolved, patient stable

Invalid transitions prevented by:
- Business logic in Nurse Triage agent
- Database constraints (check valid transitions)
- Audit logging of all status changes
```

### Authentication State Machine (Patient)

```
┌──────────┐     ┌──────────┐     ┌──────────┐
│  START   │────►│ ENTERED  │────►│ VERIFIED │
│          │     │   CODE   │     │          │
└──────────┘     └──────────┘     └──────────┘
      │                  │
      │                  ▼
      │            ┌──────────┐
      │            │ ENTERED  │
      │            │   DOB    │
      │            └──────────┘
      │                  │
      ▼                  ▼
┌──────────┐     ┌──────────┐
│  FAILED  │◄────│  LOCKED  │
│ (3 tries)│     │ (15 min) │
└──────────┘     └──────────┘
```

## Security Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     SECURITY BOUNDARIES                          │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │                    PUBLIC INTERNET                       │   │
│  │  ┌─────────┐  ┌─────────┐  ┌─────────┐                  │   │
│  │  │  SMS    │  │  Voice  │  │  Web    │                  │   │
│  │  │ (Twilio)│  │ (Twilio)│  │ (HTTPS) │                  │   │
│  │  └────┬────┘  └────┬────┘  └────┬────┘                  │   │
│  └───────┼────────────┼──────────┼────────────────────────┘   │
│          │            │          │                              │
│          └────────────┴──────────┘                              │
│                     │                                            │
│                     ▼                                            │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │                 APPLICATION LAYER (Django)                 │   │
│  │  ┌─────────┐  ┌─────────┐  ┌─────────┐  ┌─────────┐      │   │
│  │  │  Auth   │  │  API    │  │ WebSocket│  │  Admin  │      │   │
│  │  │Middleware│  │Endpoints│  │Handlers │  │  Panel  │      │   │
│  │  └────┬────┘  └────┬────┘  └────┬────┘  └────┬────┘      │   │
│  └───────┼────────────┼──────────┼────────────┼───────────┘   │
│          │            │          │              │               │
│          └────────────┴──────────┴──────────────┘               │
│                     │                                            │
│                     ▼                                            │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │                   DATA LAYER (PostgreSQL)                  │   │
│  │  ┌─────────┐  ┌─────────┐  ┌─────────┐  ┌─────────┐      │   │
│  │  │ Patients│  │ Messages│  │  Audit  │  │ Clinical│      │   │
│  │  │  Table  │  │  Table  │  │   Logs  │  │ Content │      │   │
│  │  └─────────┘  └─────────┘  └─────────┘  └─────────┘      │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                  │
│  ACCESS CONTROL:                                                 │
│  • Patients: Own data only                                       │
│  • Caregivers: Patient data with consent                        │
│  • Clinicians: Assigned patients + escalations                  │
│  • Admins: Aggregate metrics only (no PHI)                      │
│                                                                  │
│  SECURITY MEASURES:                                              │
│  • All data encrypted at rest (PostgreSQL TDE)                 │
│  • All connections over TLS 1.3                                 │
│  • API rate limiting per user                                   │
│  • Input validation and sanitization                            │
│  • SQL injection prevention (Django ORM)                        │
│  • XSS protection (Django templates)                            │
│  • CSRF protection on all state-changing requests               │
│  • File upload validation (type, size, content)                  │
│                                                                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Key Architectural Decisions

### Decision 1: Agent State Management
**Status:** ✅ Resolved
**Decision:** PostgreSQL with conversation state table
**Rationale:** Provides durability, auditability, and scales horizontally—critical for healthcare compliance. Enables conversation recovery after server restart and supports horizontal scaling of agent workers.

### Decision 2: Real-Time Updates Architecture
**Status:** ✅ Resolved
**Decision:** WebSockets + persistent notification queue
**Rationale:** Ensures no critical alerts are missed even if WebSocket disconnects. Notifications queue in PostgreSQL and retry until acknowledged, essential for patient safety.

### Decision 3: LLM Response Time & Timeouts
**Status:** ✅ Resolved
**Decision:** Typing indicator with synchronous responses
**Rationale:** Provides immediate feedback to patients, feels more human, and allows time for tool invocation in background. Better for elderly patients who expect conversational flow.

### Decision 4: Multi-Tenancy (Hospital Isolation)
**Status:** ✅ Resolved
**Decision:** Row-level tenancy with hospital_id
**Rationale:** Allows shared infrastructure with data isolation. Easier operations than separate databases per hospital while maintaining strict data boundaries.

### Decision 5: Audit Logging Strategy
**Status:** ✅ Resolved
**Decision:** Structured audit events table + append-only log
**Rationale:** Captures clinically significant actions for compliance while avoiding noise from routine queries. Tamper-evident storage meets HIPAA requirements.

### Decision 6: File Upload Security (Voice Memos)
**Status:** ✅ Resolved
**Decision:** Strict validation + isolated storage + scanning
**Rationale:** Healthcare data requires defense in depth. Audio uploads are validated for type and size, stored in isolated bucket, and scanned for malware.

### Decision 7: Database Schema for Agent Conversations
**Status:** ✅ Resolved
**Decision:** Hybrid: normalized core + JSONB for agent metadata
**Rationale:** Balances queryability for common operations with flexibility for agent-specific data that changes frequently. Messages table is normalized; agent context and tool calls stored in JSONB.

---

## Database Schema Strategy

### Core Tables (Normalized)

```sql
-- Multi-tenancy
CREATE TABLE hospitals (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    timezone VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'America/New_York',
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Patients
CREATE TABLE patients (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    hospital_id UUID REFERENCES hospitals(id),
    phone_number VARCHAR(20) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    date_of_birth DATE NOT NULL,
    name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    preferred_language VARCHAR(10) DEFAULT 'en',
    preferred_timezone VARCHAR(50),
    status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'green' CHECK (status IN ('green', 'yellow', 'orange', 'red', 'resolved')),
    leaflet_code VARCHAR(4),
    leaflet_code_expires_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_patients_hospital_id ON patients(hospital_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_patients_status_hospital ON patients(status, hospital_id);

-- Caregivers
CREATE TABLE caregivers (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    patient_id UUID REFERENCES patients(id),
    phone_number VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
    name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    relationship VARCHAR(100),
    consent_status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK (consent_status IN ('pending', 'active', 'revoked')),
    consent_granted_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Messages (normalized core)
CREATE TABLE messages (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    patient_id UUID REFERENCES patients(id),
    sender_type VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL CHECK (sender_type IN ('patient', 'agent', 'caregiver', 'clinician')),
    sender_id UUID NOT NULL,
    content TEXT NOT NULL,
    channel VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL CHECK (channel IN ('sms', 'web', 'voice')),
    direction VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL CHECK (direction IN ('inbound', 'outbound')),
    status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'delivered', 'failed')),
    external_id VARCHAR(255), -- Twilio message SID
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_messages_patient_created ON messages(patient_id, created_at);

-- Clinicians
CREATE TABLE clinicians (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    hospital_id UUID REFERENCES hospitals(id),
    email VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    role VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL CHECK (role IN ('nurse', 'physician', 'admin')),
    is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

### Agent Tables (Hybrid with JSONB)

```sql
-- Agent conversations
CREATE TABLE agent_conversations (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    patient_id UUID REFERENCES patients(id),
    agent_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'active' CHECK (status IN ('active', 'paused', 'completed')),

    -- JSONB for flexible agent context
    context JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
    -- Example: {"patient_summary": "...", "recent_symptoms": [...], "tools_invoked": [...]}

    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Agent messages (link to messages table + add agent metadata)
CREATE TABLE agent_messages (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    message_id UUID REFERENCES messages(id),
    conversation_id UUID REFERENCES agent_conversations(id),

    -- JSONB for agent-specific data
    metadata JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
    -- Example: {"llm_prompt": "...", "llm_response": "...", "tokens_used": 150, "latency_ms": 2500}

    tool_invocations JSONB DEFAULT '[]',
    -- Example: [{"tool": "symptom_checker", "input": {...}, "output": {...}}]

    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

### Audit Tables

```sql
-- Audit events
CREATE TABLE audit_events (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    hospital_id UUID REFERENCES hospitals(id),
    user_id UUID NOT NULL,
    user_type VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL CHECK (user_type IN ('patient', 'caregiver', 'clinician', 'system')),
    action VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    resource_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    resource_id UUID,
    details JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
    ip_address INET,
    user_agent TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_audit_events_user_time ON audit_events(user_id, created_at);
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_events_hospital_time ON audit_events(hospital_id, created_at);

-- Notification queue (for WebSocket fallback)
CREATE TABLE notification_queue (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    hospital_id UUID REFERENCES hospitals(id),
    user_id UUID NOT NULL,
    user_type VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
    event_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    payload JSONB NOT NULL,
    status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'delivered', 'failed')),
    retry_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
    delivered_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE
);

CREATE INDEX idx_notification_queue_status ON notification_queue(status) WHERE status = 'pending';
```

---

## Test Plan

### Test Coverage Requirements

**Unit Tests (>90% coverage required):**
- Authentication logic (leaflet codes, DOB parsing, lockout)
- Agent routing and orchestration
- Multi-tenancy data scoping
- Timezone conversions
- Status state machine transitions

**Integration Tests:**
- Full patient message flow (send → agent → response)
- WebSocket real-time updates
- File upload and transcription
- Caregiver invitation and access
- Escalation notification chain

**E2E Tests:**
- Patient onboarding complete flow
- Clinician dashboard critical path
- Emergency escalation flow

### Critical Paths (Must Test)

#### Path 1: Patient Emergency Flow
```
Patient reports severe symptom
    ↓
Nurse Triage agent evaluates
    ↓
Status escalates to RED
    ↓
Real-time alert to clinician
    ↓
Escalation notification sent
    ↓
Clinician acknowledges
```

**Test scenarios:**
- Happy path: All steps complete successfully
- LLM timeout: Fallback to human escalation
- WebSocket disconnect: Notification queue delivers alert
- Clinician offline: Escalation chain to next clinician

#### Path 2: Caregiver Consent Flow
```
Patient invites caregiver
    ↓
Caregiver receives invitation
    ↓
Caregiver accepts with DOB verification
    ↓
Consent recorded in DB
    ↓
Caregiver views patient data (read-only)
```

**Test scenarios:**
- Happy path: Full consent flow
- Invalid DOB: Rejection with clear error
- Expired invitation: Cannot accept after expiration
- Revoked consent: Caregiver loses access immediately

#### Path 3: Audit Compliance
```
Any patient data access
    ↓
Audit log entry created
    ↓
Log immutable and queryable
    ↓
Report generated for compliance
```

**Test scenarios:**
- All access types logged (read, write, delete)
- Log entries cannot be modified
- Query by user, patient, time range
- Export for compliance review

### LLM/Prompt Testing

Since this uses LangChain/LangGraph with Ollama Cloud:

**Eval Suite Required:**
- **Safety evals**: Ensure agents don't give medical advice beyond scope
- **Tone evals**: Verify supportive, non-clinical language for patients
- **Routing evals**: Test supervisor correctly routes to appropriate agent
- **Tool use evals**: Verify agents invoke tools correctly
- **Fallback testing**: Test behavior when LLM is unavailable

**Golden Examples:**
- 50 example patient messages with expected agent responses
- 20 edge cases (ambiguous symptoms, emotional distress, etc.)
- 10 safety test cases (requests for medical advice, emergency situations)

---

## Failure Modes Registry

| CODEPATH | FAILURE MODE | RESCUED? | TEST? | USER SEES? | LOGGED? |
|----------|--------------|----------|-------|------------|---------|
| LLM API call | Timeout | Retry + fallback | Yes | "Let me connect you with a nurse" | Yes |
| Twilio send | Delivery failure | Queue + retry | Yes | Transparent (queued) | Yes |
| DOB validation | Invalid format | Parse attempts | Yes | "Please check your date of birth" | Yes |
| WebSocket | Disconnect | Reconnect logic | Yes | Auto-reconnect | Yes |
| File upload | Invalid type/size | Validation reject | Yes | Clear error message | Yes |
| Agent routing | Ambiguous intent | Clarification prompt | Yes | "Can you tell me more about..." | Yes |
| Status escalation | System error | Manual fallback | Yes | "Please call your care team" | Yes |
| Database | Connection lost | Retry + circuit breaker | Yes | "Service temporarily unavailable" | Yes |

**Critical gaps:** None identified. All failure modes have rescue paths, tests, user feedback, and logging.

---

## Performance Considerations

### Potential Bottlenecks

1. **LLM API Latency**: 2-5 second response times
   - **Mitigation**: Typing indicator, connection pooling, async processing for non-urgent tasks
   - **Monitoring**: Track p95 and p99 latency, alert on >5s

2. **WebSocket Broadcasts**: Many clinicians watching same patient
   - **Mitigation**: Efficient channel groups, message deduplication
   - **Monitoring**: Track broadcast latency, connection count

3. **Patient List Queries**: Clinicians with 100+ patients
   - **Mitigation**: Pagination, eager loading, database indexes
   - **Monitoring**: Query execution time, page load time

4. **Audit Log Writes**: High volume of events
   - **Mitigation**: Async writes, batching, separate audit database if needed
   - **Monitoring**: Write queue depth, lag time

### Database Indexes

```sql
-- Multi-tenancy
CREATE INDEX idx_patients_hospital_id ON patients(hospital_id);

-- Status queries for triage
CREATE INDEX idx_patients_status_hospital ON patients(status, hospital_id);

-- Conversation lookups
CREATE INDEX idx_messages_patient_created ON messages(patient_id, created_at);

-- Audit queries
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_events_user_time ON audit_events(user_id, created_at);

-- Notification queue
CREATE INDEX idx_notification_queue_status ON notification_queue(status) WHERE status = 'pending';
```

### Caching Strategy

- **Patient profiles**: Cache for 5 minutes (rarely change)
- **Clinical pathways**: Cache for 1 hour (versioned content)
- **Active conversations**: No caching (must be real-time)
- **Dashboard metrics**: Cache for 1 minute (balance freshness vs. load)

### Phase 1: Foundation (Weeks 1-2)

- [ ] Core models (Hospital, Patient, Clinician)
- [ ] Authentication system (leaflet codes + DOB)

- [ ] Database migrations and indexes

### Phase 2: Agent System (Weeks 3-4)

- [ ] Supervisor agent implementation
- [ ] Care Coordinator agent (basic)
- [ ] Conversation state persistence
- [ ] Agent message logging

### Phase 3: Communication (Weeks 5-6)

- [ ] WebSocket setup for real-time updates
- [ ] Notification queue implementation
- [ ] Voice memo upload and storage
- [ ] Basic transcription (placeholder)

### Phase 4: Clinical Features (Weeks 7-8)

### Phase 6: Polish & Testing (Weeks 11-12)

- [ ] Comprehensive test suite
